title: Spergella subulata
intro: Spergella subulata, also known as Lesser Sandspurry, is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its prostrate growth habit and tiny, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Spergella subulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Spergella subulata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Spergella subulata belongs to the family Brassicaceae and is a member of the genus Spergella. It is a small, annual herb native to Europe and Western Asia. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, Spergella subulata is closely related to other plants like cabbages and mustards. The genus Spergella is a group of small annual herbs commonly known as sandworts. Spergella subulata is further classified as a species within the Spergella genus.
